title: LakehousePlumber Pipeline Configuration
description: Schema for one YAML document of the multi-document pipeline configuration file (config/pipeline_config*.yaml). Each document is either a project_defaults document (applies to ALL pipelines) or a pipeline-specific document keyed by 'pipeline' (string or list of pipeline names). Merge order: built-in defaults (serverless: true, edition: ADVANCED, channel: CURRENT, continuous: false) -> project_defaults -> pipeline-specific. Nested mappings are deep-merged; lists are replaced. Unknown keys are passed through to the generated bundle resource, so new Databricks pipeline fields work without an LHP release.

ProjectDefaultsDocument.description: Project-wide defaults applied to every pipeline unless overridden by a pipeline-specific document

project_defaults.description: Settings applied to ALL pipelines unless overridden by a pipeline-specific document

pipeline.description: Pipeline name(s) this document configures. A list applies the same configuration to multiple pipelines (each gets an independent deep copy). Use the standalone alias '__eventlog_monitoring' to configure the event-log monitoring pipeline without knowing its resolved name.

oneOf.description: List of pipeline names sharing this configuration

PipelineSettings.description: Pipeline settings. Every value supports LHP substitution tokens (e.g. ${catalog}). Unknown keys are passed through to the generated bundle resource unchanged.

serverless.description: Use serverless compute. When false, a clusters configuration is required.

edition.description: Pipeline edition (only meaningful when serverless: false)

channel.description: Runtime channel: CURRENT (stable) or PREVIEW (latest features)

continuous.description: Continuous (streaming) mode; false runs the pipeline in triggered mode

packaging.description: How generated code is deployed: 'source' (notebooks/files) or 'wheel' (built wheel; requires wheel.artifact_volume in lhp.yaml)

catalog.description: Unity Catalog catalog for the pipeline. Must be set together with 'schema' (in project_defaults or per pipeline). Supports LHP substitution tokens.

schema.description: Unity Catalog schema for the pipeline. Must be set together with 'catalog'. Supports LHP substitution tokens.

configuration.description: Arbitrary pipeline-level Spark/pipeline configuration entries. All values must be strings (quote them).

environment.description: Pipeline environment specification (e.g. dependencies for serverless pipelines)

permissions.description: Pipeline-level access control entries. Each entry needs a 'level' and exactly one identity (user_name, group_name, or service_principal_name).

level.description: Permission level (e.g. CAN_VIEW, CAN_RUN, CAN_MANAGE, CAN_MANAGE_RUN)

user_name.description: User email this entry applies to

group_name.description: Group name this entry applies to

service_principal_name.description: Service principal application ID this entry applies to

clusters.description: Cluster configuration (required when serverless: false). Passed through to the generated bundle resource.

notifications.description: Email notification blocks (email_recipients + alerts). Passed through to the generated bundle resource; replaces (not appends to) any inherited value.

tags.description: Custom tags for cost tracking and organization (non-serverless pipelines). Passed through to the generated bundle resource.

photon.description: Enable the Photon engine (only meaningful when serverless: false). Passed through to the generated bundle resource.

event_log.description: Per-pipeline event log override. An object fully replaces the lhp.yaml event_log setting for this pipeline; 'false' disables event logging for this pipeline only.

run_as.description: Identity the pipeline runs as (e.g. service_principal_name). Passed through to the generated bundle resource.
